get.summary: GET /ready — checks DB connectivity (and Redis if configured).

503.description: One or more readiness checks failed

parameters.name: return_to
parameters.description: Same-origin app path to return to

303.description: Redirect back to app with session cookie

delete.description:
Password-confirmed. Blocks (409) while the caller still owns any
organization that has other active members — they must transfer
ownership first. Otherwise: soft-deletes organizations where the caller
was the sole member, removes the caller's memberships, and anonymizes
the `users` row (PII scrubbed, `deleted_at` set). Research
sessions/responses are retained **pseudonymously** — the `user_id` link
is left intact so longitudinal/aggregate analysis survives while the
personal data on the users row is erased. Refresh tokens are revoked
after the request transaction commits.

409.description: Still owns shared organization(s); transfer ownership first

200.description: Organizations available to the current user

get.description:
Anonymous, lightweight branding read (E-RBAC-8). The participant fillout
chrome fetches this to theme itself. `organizations` is RLS-exempt (ADR 0015),
so this queries the pool directly like the public by-code questionnaire read —
no auth, no per-request tx. Returns only presentation fields.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User id whose role is changing

400.description: Invalid role or last-owner guard tripped

post.description:
The sanctioned, audited path to hand an organization to another member.
Unlike `change_member_role` — which refuses to demote the last owner —
this promotes the new owner *and* (by default) demotes the caller in one
transaction, so the org is never left ownerless. The caller must be a
current owner and re-confirm their password.

400.description: Target is not a member / would leave no owner

get.summary:
GET /api/invitations/pending
Returns all pending invitations addressed to the authenticated user's email.

200.description: Pending invitations for the current user

404.description: Invitation not found or already processed
Invitation not found or already processed

200.description: Auto-join resolution for an email address

get.description:
Reverse-chronological, keyset-paginated audit timeline for one
organization. Org admin/owner gated (defense-in-depth: the
`audit_events_select` RLS policy also admits only active admins/owners).

parameters.name: actor
parameters.description: Filter to a single actor user id.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque keyset cursor from a prior page's `next_cursor`.

post.description:
Owner-gated. Enqueues an async export job: inserts a `pending`
`data_exports` row, spawns a background task that serializes the whole
tenant into a zip in object storage, and returns the job immediately.
Refuses (409) when an export is already in flight for the org (belt to the
route's rate-limit layer).

409.description: An export is already in progress

get.description:
Admin/owner-gated. Reports job status and, once the artifact is `ready` and
unexpired, hands back a short-lived presigned download URL. A ready artifact
past its TTL is reported as `expired` with no URL.

post.description:
Owner-gated, password + typed-confirmation guarded, blocked under a legal
hold. Removes the tenant's participant data (deleting the org's projects
cascades through questionnaires → sessions → responses / events / variables
/ media rows) and soft-deletes the organization, retaining `audit_events`.

put.description:
Owner-gated. Sets the data-residency region, allowed ONLY while the org
owns no data yet (no projects, no sessions) — a residency commitment is
immutable once data has landed under the current region's storage prefix.
Owner-gated. Enables or releases the legal hold that blocks tenant erasure.

409.description: A role with that name already exists

403.description: Access denied or system role is immutable
Access denied or system role is immutable

put.description:
Assign (or clear, with `custom_role_id: null`) a member's custom role.
The referenced role must belong to the same org.

get.description:
Anonymous. Resolves whether the email's verified domain (P8-T8
`organization_domains`) belongs to an org with an enabled IdP, so the login
page can offer a "Sign in with SSO" affordance and know which org slug to
hand to `start`.

200.description: SSO availability for the email's domain

get.description:
Anonymous. Resolves the org's enabled IdP, fetches OIDC discovery, records a
single-use login-state row (CSRF `state` + `nonce`, pinned endpoints), and
redirects the browser to the IdP authorization endpoint.

404.description: No SSO configured for this organization

get.description:
Anonymous. Completes the authorization-code exchange, validates the
id_token, JIT-provisions the user + membership, mints an opaque app session,
and bounces the browser back to the SPA. Browser-visible tokens are not
returned from this flow.

303.description: Redirect back to the app with a session

201.description: API key created — plaintext shown once
SCIM token created — plaintext shown once

200.description: Projects visible to the current user

post.description:
Reassigns the sole project owner. The caller must be the current project
owner or an org owner/admin. The new owner must already be an org member;
if they are not yet a project member they are auto-added as `owner`. Any
previous project owner is demoted to `admin` in the same transaction.

400.description: Target is not a member / would leave no owner

403.description: Caller may not transfer this project

200.description: Question templates available in the organization

post.summary: POST /api/sessions — create a new session (can be anonymous).

403.description: Questionnaire not published or access denied

get.description:
Live per-arm allocation counts straight from the `arm_counts` ledger
(E-FLOW-6, dovetails with the analytics per-arm readout / P8-T4). This is
the authoritative assignment tally maintained atomically at create time —
distinct from `condition-counts`, which derives from session rows.

Returns the current completion counts for quota management.
The client evaluates quota conditions against these counts.

Returns live per-cell occupancy for interlocking cross-quota cells. The
client computes the participant's cell key from live in-survey variables
and blocks only when THAT cell is full (independent-cell semantics), rather
than when any sibling cell is full.

Anonymous, like `quota_status`. `quota_cells` carries no RLS policy and is
aggregate-only (no per-session rows), so a plain SELECT under the app role
is safe — no SECURITY DEFINER needed for the read. A cell only appears once
it has received its first claim; a not-yet-claimed cell is absent (occupancy
0), which the client treats as "room available".

Public, aggregate-only cohort statistics for a PUBLISHED questionnaire.
This is the anonymous-safe cohort feedback path (F060): the
statistical-feedback module's `cohort` / `participant-vs-cohort` modes call
it from the fillout runtime, where the caller is an anonymous participant
who can never reach the `AuthenticatedUser`-gated `/api/sessions/aggregate`.

Only aggregates cross the wire — the underlying `fillout_cohort_stats`
SECURITY DEFINER function computes moments/percentiles over COMPLETED
sessions and never returns a per-session value. A min-N floor is applied
here: when `n < 5` the response reports the count with ALL stats null,
blocking small-cohort deanonymization. Unpublished / missing questionnaire
→ 404.

200.description: Aggregate-only cohort stats (null stats when n < 5)

404.description: Questionnaire not found or not published

get.description:
Anonymous-safe batch of SERVER-COMPUTED VARIABLE aggregates
(server-computed-variable / E-FEEDBACK-3). Structured like
[`public_cohort_stats`]: a published-questionnaire gate (unpublished/missing
→ 404 without existence probing), then a resolve of the definition JSONB
(the latest published registry, or a pinned `questionnaire_snapshots` row
when `?version=` is supplied), then — for each `server` declaration on
`content.variables` (capped at 50) — one `fillout_dataset_stats` call over
COMPLETED sessions via the SECURITY DEFINER function.

THIS is the authorization model: the anonymous client sends zero filter data;
only the designer-published declarations are ever evaluated. The MIN_COHORT_N
floor applies per entry (n < 5 → count reported, stats withheld). Results are
cached (Redis, in-memory fallback) keyed `srvvars:{qid}:{version}` for
`SRV_VARS_TTL_SECS`, which also paces the auto-recalculation.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description:
Optional `major.minor.patch` selecting a pinned questionnaire snapshot.
Absent → the latest published registry definition.

200.description: Server-computed variable aggregates (stats null below the n<5 floor)

404.description: Questionnaire not found or not published

get.description:
Lightweight reconcile probe (E-OFF-5): returns the `client_id`s the server
durably holds for the session (responses + interaction events + trials). The client
diffs its locally-`acked` ledger rows against this set and re-queues anything
the server does not actually have — defending against a client-side
over-marking that would otherwise strand data. Runs under the fillout GUC
(`app.session_id` from the URL path), so an anonymous session can probe its
own durability; `ensure_session_participant_or_member` gates access.

200.description: Client ids the server durably holds
Media asset with a presigned URL

get.description:
Streams the S3 object bytes on the API origin instead of redirecting to
an expiring presigned MinIO URL. This is what fixes cross-origin WebGL
texture taint, COEP `require-corp` on the fillout route, and the
Cache-API keying problem (a presigned URL expires and re-issues, so it
can't be a durable cache key).

Authorization is by RLS admission, mirroring the by-code anonymous-read
posture (ADR 0012/0015): this route runs under `set_fillout_rls_context`
(optional JWT), and the `SELECT` below is admitted either by the 00014
`media_assets_select` org-member policy (authenticated project member)
or by the 00025 `media_assets_select_via_published_questionnaire` policy
(asset referenced by a published questionnaire — anonymous fillout). A
non-admitted row simply isn't returned → 404.

Honors a single `Range` (`bytes=start-end`) by passing it through to S3
and replying `206` with `Content-Range`; otherwise `200` full body.
The response carries `Content-Type` (from the stored mime), an `ETag`
(the immutable storage key), `Accept-Ranges: bytes`, and
`Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`.

post.summary:
POST /api/sessions/:id/media — anonymous multipart upload scoped to a session.
No JWT required; validates the session exists and is active.

get.summary: GET /api/series?questionnaire_id=… — list series for a questionnaire.

post.summary: POST /api/series — create a study series for a questionnaire.

patch.summary: PATCH /api/series/{id} — update series definition / reminder copy.

post.summary:
POST /api/series/{id}/enroll — enroll a participant and materialize the
prompt window (all waves for fixed/random; wave 0 for event).

get.summary: GET /api/series/{id}/enrollments — list enrollments for a series.

GET /api/series/prompt/{resume_token} — resolve a reminder link to the
current wave (+ pinned version + `_waveIndex` / `_seriesElapsedDays`).
Marks the current wave's prompt opened.

post.summary:
POST /api/series/prompt/{resume_token}/complete — advance the enrollment
after a wave is completed. Binds the session to the wave prompt and
schedules the next wave (materialized already for fixed/random; computed
now for event).

POST /api/series/prompt/{resume_token}/unsubscribe — opt out. Flips the
enrollment to `withdrawn`; the scheduler stops sending (its due scan
filters `status='active'`).

ApiKeyRecord.description:
One API key with the secret redacted. `revoked` is a convenience flag
derived from `revoked_at`.

prefix.description: The public lookup handle (embedded verbatim in the plaintext token).

ArmCount.description:
A single live per-arm count row for the designer readout
(`GET /api/questionnaires/{id}/arm-counts`).

custom_role_id.description:
The custom role to assign, or `null` to clear the assignment (demote
the member back to their system-role defaults).

AuditEventRecord.description: One row of the org audit timeline, joined with the actor's identity.

ip.description: Actor IP as a plain host string (no netmask), or null.

AuditListResponse.description:
Paginated audit-timeline page. `next_cursor` is present only when more
rows may exist (the page came back full); pass it back as `cursor`.

session_id.description: The fillout session that completed this wave.

wave_index.description:
The wave that was completed. Defaults to the enrollment's current
wave when omitted.

status.description:
Enrollment status after advancing: `active` (more waves) or
`completed` (series finished).

expires_at.description: Optional hard expiry; omit for a non-expiring key.

CreateApiKeyResponse.description:
Returned exactly once on creation — the only time the plaintext `key` is
ever available. Store it now or it is unrecoverable.

key.description: The full plaintext token `sk_<prefix>_<secret>`. Shown ONCE.

metadata_url.description: OIDC discovery URL / SAML metadata URL (reachability validated).

client_secret.description: Plaintext client secret; stored encrypted, never returned.

group_role_map.description: Object mapping IdP group name → org role (`admin`|`member`|`viewer`).

data_region.description:
Data-residency region (E-RBAC-9). Chosen at creation and immutable in-app
once the org owns data. Defaults to `eu` when omitted.

CreateScimTokenResponse.description: Returned once on creation — the plaintext `token` is unrecoverable after.

token.description: The full plaintext SCIM bearer token. Shown ONCE.

fingerprint.description:
Client-generated browser fingerprint hash used for create-time
duplicate-participation detection. Optional; when omitted we fall
back to `metadata->>'fingerprint'` for backward compatibility.

resume_token.description:
E-FLOW-2: when this session materializes a longitudinal/EMA series
wave (opened from a reminder link), the enrollment's `resume_token`.
Stored on `sessions.resume_token` so the wave binds back to its
prompt in the dataset.

participant_number.description:
0-based monotonic per-questionnaire participant index allocated at
create time (E-FLOW-6). The client seeds counterbalancing
(`getBlockOrder`) and offline condition assignment with this, so
Latin-square rows actually rotate across participants.

assigned_condition.description:
Server-authoritative between-subjects arm assignment (E-FLOW-6),
claimed atomically against `arm_counts`. Absent (null) when the
questionnaire declares no experimental design, or when every arm is at
cap. The client prefers this over local `ConditionAssigner` when present.

CreateSessionResponse.description:
Response for `POST /api/sessions`. Flattens the created [`Session`] and
adds `duplicate` — true when a prior COMPLETED session for the same
questionnaire shares the caller-provided fingerprint. The session is
created regardless; the client decides whether to warn or block per the
questionnaire's fraud-prevention settings (repeat participation may be
allowed, so the server does not hard-block here).

password.description: Current account password, re-confirmed to authorize the erasure.

contact_channel.description: Delivery address for `channel_kind` (an email address for `email`).

channel_kind.description: Only `email` is supported today. Defaults to `email`.

resume_link.description: Absolute participant resume link (also delivered by every reminder).

materialized_waves.description:
Number of prompts materialized at enrollment (all waves for
fixed/random schedules; 1 for event schedules).

password.description:
Caller's current password, re-confirmed to authorize the destructive
action.

confirmation.description:
Typed confirmation — must equal the organization's slug (case
insensitive), the standard "type the name to confirm" guard.

EraseResult.description: Summary returned by the erase endpoint.

ExportJob.description: Public view of a `data_exports` job row.

download_url.description:
Short-lived presigned download URL — present only when the artifact is
ready and unexpired.

error.description: Operator-facing failure reason, present only on a failed job.

IdentityProviderRecord.description:
One identity provider, with the `client_secret` redacted to a presence
flag. Never carries the ciphertext or plaintext secret.

has_client_secret.description: True when a `client_secret` is stored (its value is never returned).

width.description:
Intrinsic pixel width for image assets; NULL for non-images or
headers that could not be parsed (F-8).

height.description:
Intrinsic pixel height for image assets; NULL for non-images or
headers that could not be parsed (F-8).

OrgBranding.description:
Lightweight, anonymously-readable branding for an organization (E-RBAC-8).
The participant fillout chrome fetches this to theme itself. Non-sensitive
by construction — only presentation fields, resolved with platform fallbacks
on the client.

primary_color.description: Brand primary color (`settings.primaryColor`), or null for the platform default.

logo_url.description: Logo URL — `settings.logoUrl` if set, else the legacy `logo_url` column.

custom_role_id.description:
Assigned custom role (E-RBAC-3), if any — its permissions override the
`role` tier's defaults for this member.

OrgRoleRecord.description: One role definition (system preset or custom).

OrgSettings.description:
Documented shape of `organizations.settings` (E-RBAC-8, ADR trail).

The column stays free-form JSONB — this struct is a *typed view* over the
keys the platform cares about, used to validate writes ([`validate_org_settings`])
and to surface branding anonymously ([`get_org_branding`]). Unknown keys
(e.g. the `defaults` block the settings UI stores) deserialize away and are
preserved on write because `update_organization` persists the caller's whole
object, not a re-serialization of this struct. Keys are camelCase to match the
JSON the frontend writes and the `settings->>'seatLimit'` reads in [`seat_usage`].

primaryColor.description:
Brand primary color as a CSS hex string (`#RGB` or `#RRGGBB`). Applied to
participant chrome as the `--primary` token.

logoUrl.description: Absolute http(s) or same-origin logo URL rendered on participant chrome.

participantHeader.description: Short header text shown above participant questionnaires.

projectVisibility.description: Default visibility for newly created projects (E-RBAC-1).

idpManagedRoles.description:
When true, org member roles are managed by the IdP and not editable
in-app (E-RBAC-6).

data_region.description:
Data-residency region tag (E-RBAC-9), e.g. `eu`/`us`. Threaded into
storage key prefixes; immutable in-app once the org owns data.

legal_hold.description: When true, destructive tenant erasure is blocked (E-RBAC-9).

PendingInvitation.description: Model returned for pending-invitation list (includes org context).

questionnaire_count.description:
Number of non-deleted questionnaires in this project. Populated by the
list handler's aggregate subquery; absent (defaults to `None`) on the
single-row get/create/update paths.

organization_id.description:
Owning organization id — lets the anonymous fillout route fetch org
branding (`GET /api/organizations/{id}/branding`, E-RBAC-8) to theme the
participant chrome.

QuotaCellStatus.description:
Live occupancy of a single interlocking quota cell (E-FLOW-7). The client
selects the participant's cell by key and blocks only when that cell is full.

RolesListResponse.description:
The full catalogue of assignable permission tokens — returned alongside
the roles so the matrix editor can render the checkbox grid without
hard-coding the list client-side.

ScimTokenRecord.description: One SCIM bearer token with the secret redacted.

SeatUsageResponse.description:
Seat usage for an organization. `limit` is null when no `seatLimit` is set
in `organizations.settings` (unlimited). A "seat" is an active member or a
pending invitation (an outstanding invitation reserves a prospective seat).

limit.description: Configured seat limit (`settings->>'seatLimit'`), or null when unlimited.

SeriesPromptResolution.description:
What the participant client needs to open the correct wave from a
reminder link. `wave_index` + `series_elapsed_days` are exposed to the
runtime as the `_waveIndex` / `_seriesElapsedDays` flow variables.

scheduled_at.description:
When the CURRENT wave's prompt is scheduled (for the "come back
later" state when it is in the future).

next_prompt_at.description: When the NEXT wave is due (participant "come back later" copy).

ServerVariableEntry.description: One computed server-variable aggregate in the `/server-variables` response.

name.description: Variable name (the mathjs symbol consumers resolve).

key.description: Session variable name or question id being aggregated.

dataset_id.description: Declared dataset-filter id, when the declaration carries one.

decl_hash.description:
Stable content hash of the `server` declaration (matches the TS
`declHash`), so the client can cross-check its local declaration and
safely reuse a cached row cross-version when byte-identical.

sample_count.description: Cohort size (populated even below the anonymity floor).

oneOf.description: Full stats, or `None` when withheld below the MIN_COHORT_N floor.

version.description: The `major.minor.patch` the aggregates were computed against.

fallback_registry.description:
True when a `?version=` snapshot was requested but had been pruned, so the
latest registry declarations were evaluated instead.

trials.description:
Per-trial rows (RT-1b), the fourth offline record kind. Optional so
older clients that only sync responses/events/variables still
deserialize; defaults to empty.

oneOf.description: Present when the session may not yet exist on the server (offline-created).

timing_provenance.description:
Per-response timing-provenance blob (contract C-PROVENANCE). Sent
snake_case as `timing_provenance`; `timingProvenance` accepted as an
alias. Optional so older clients that omit it still deserialize.

trials_synced.description:
Count of `trials[]` rows freshly inserted this sync (RT-1b). Duplicates
skipped by `ON CONFLICT (client_id) DO NOTHING` are not counted, exactly
like `responses_synced`.

accepted_client_ids.description:
Ack-driven marking (E-OFF-4): the `client_id`s (responses AND events) the
server DURABLY HOLDS after this sync — i.e. every id in a chunk whose
`INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (client_id) DO NOTHING` statement committed, which
includes rows that were already present from a prior partial sync. The
client flips ONLY these rows to `synced=1`; anything absent here stays
unsynced and retries next pass. Empty when nothing was written.

accepted_variable_names.description:
Ack-driven marking (E-OFF-4): the session-variable NAMES durably upserted
by this sync. The client marks only these variable rows synced (guarded by
a per-record clientId concurrency token), replacing the old mark-all-by-
session semantics that raced with mid-flight variable writes.

SyncSessionInit.description:
Session-creation fields carried by the sync payload so a session that was
created while OFFLINE (exists only on the client) can be materialized on the
server at sync time. Optional: online sessions already exist and omit this.

SyncTrialItem.description:
One per-trial row in the sync payload (RT-1b). Fields mirror the `trials`
table columns; snake_case with camelCase aliases like the other sync items.

SyncedClientIdsResponse.description:
Response for `GET /api/sessions/{id}/synced-client-ids` (E-OFF-5 reconcile).
The `client_id`s the server DURABLY HOLDS for the session (responses +
interaction events). The client diffs its locally-`acked` ledger rows against
this set to detect and re-queue any over-marking (locally acked, server-missing).

new_owner_user_id.description: The org member who becomes the new owner. Must be an active member.

password.description: Caller's current password — required to re-confirm a sensitive action.

demote_previous_owner.description:
When true (default), the outgoing owner is demoted to `admin` in the
same transaction. When false, the org ends up with two owners.

new_owner_user_id.description:
The user who becomes the new project owner. Must be an active member
of the project's parent organization (auto-added to the project as
`owner` if not already a project member).

TrialRecord.description:
A persisted per-trial row (RT-1b). Read back by
`GET /api/sessions/{id}/trials` for the session browser's "Per Trial" tab.

client_secret.description: Non-empty → re-encrypt and replace; empty string → clear the secret.

tags.name: api_keys
tags.description: API keys / service accounts for programmatic access

tags.name: scim
tags.description: SCIM 2.0 provisioning tokens and directory sync

tags.name: sessions
tags.description: Questionnaire fillout sessions and persisted data

tags.name: series
tags.description: Longitudinal / EMA study series scheduling and enrollment
